event: Established renal failure secondary to adult polycystic kidney disease (APKD)
Recurrent urinary tract infections following renal transplantation
Admitted with dysuria and right-sided loin pain
Blood pressure 140/80 mmHg pulse 80 beats/minute temperature 38.5°C
White cell count 12.5 × 10^3/mm^3 haemoglobin 9.5 g/L platelet count 568 × 10^3/mm^3 albumin 32 g/L serum creatinine 258 μmol/L urea 15.8 mmol/L potassium 6.2 mmol/L bicarbonate 16 mmol/L
Intravenous co-amoxiclav commenced
Ultrasound scan did not demonstrate any focus of infection
E. coli sensitive to co-amoxiclav found on both urine and blood culture
Clinical condition deteriorated after a week despite antibiotic therapy
Serum blood glucose level rose to 50 mmol/L
Transferred to critical care unit antibiotics switched to piperacillin and tazobactam
CT scan demonstrated gas within both renal parenchyma and a diagnosis of bilateral emphysematous pyelonephritis was made
Intubated and ventilated placed on inotropic support
Percutaneous drainage of the collection within the right kidney performed
E. coli cultured from the minimal pus aspirated
Blood cultures grew E. coli Enterococcus and Candida
Additional anti-microbial therapy included meropenem teicoplanin and caspofungin
Repeat CT scan showed that the infection in both sides had improved
Ventilatory and inotropic requirements reduced but transplant graft function declined
Continuous veno-venous haemofiltration commenced
Bilateral nephrectomies performed
Second operation required for bleeding
Received 8 units of whole blood 4 units of fresh frozen plasma and 2 units of cryoprecipitate
Patient's condition improved within 48 h of the initial operation
Transplant function recovered organ support not required beyond the sixth post-operative day
Histological examination of the kidneys revealed inflammatory changes consistent with infection
Discharged home 2 months post-operatively with a serum creatinine of 168 μmol/L
Continues to have occasional urinary tract infections
Diabetes well controlled on metformin
Serum creatinine 200 μmol/L 18 months later
